spec.title: Smoke test — every art form, every shape
spec.lede: Exercises raw SVG, named wireframes, every DSL primitive, auto-art defaults, explicit no-art suppression, and the v0.8.0 composition recipe. Theme should auto-derive per repo.

paragraphs.text: Visual previews come from {{namedlib}} and {{dslparams}}; when nothing fits, the agent can rely on the {{autoart}} default or {{hidden}} the art slot entirely. The synthesis lives in {{composed}}: raw <svg> that embeds library + DSL primitives via <use href='…'/> + custom labels + annotation marks.

squizzes.title: Named library: wf:* references
squizzes.desc: All from the registry — each option is one finished library glyph.

options.name: calendar-grid
options.recommendation: Most familiar pattern — almost every user has used GitHub's contribution graph or a calendar widget. Dense, scannable, no learning curve.

squizzes.title: DSL primitives
squizzes.desc: Each option exercises one DSL form in isolation.

squizzes.title: Auto-art when omitted
squizzes.desc: No art field set — the renderer falls back to per-letter abstract patterns. This is what happens when you SKIP authoring art; production usage should compose instead (see the composed squiz at the end).

squizzes.title: Explicit no-art (art:"none")
squizzes.desc: These options have art:"none" — the art slot should be hidden entirely (cards collapse).

squizzes.title: Composition recipe (v0.8.0)
squizzes.desc: Each option is a bespoke <svg> with embedded <use href='wf:…'/> + <use href='arch:…'/> + a labeled arrow + an annotation primitive. This is the canonical authoring shape; the previous squizzes show the parts in isolation.

options.name: labeled-object
options.desc: A library glyph + a callout marking the part that matters.
options.art: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><use href='wf:phone-card' x='5' y='0' width='55' height='60'/><use href='callout:"tap target"@70,18->58,32'/><text x='95' y='55' text-anchor='end' font-family='IBM Plex Mono' font-size='6' fill='var(--ink-2)'>card-front</text></svg>
options.recommendation: Composition is the production shape — agents authoring real squizzes should reach for this pattern, not the per-form catalogs above.

options.name: flow
options.desc: arch:* nodes wired with custom arrows + a tick badge marking the verified hop.
options.art: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><use href='arch:user' x='2' y='14' width='28' height='32'/><use href='arch:api' x='36' y='14' width='28' height='32'/><use href='arch:database' x='70' y='14' width='28' height='32'/><line x1='30' y1='30' x2='36' y2='30' stroke='var(--ink)' stroke-width='0.8'/><polygon points='36,30 33,28 33,32' fill='var(--ink)'/><line x1='64' y1='30' x2='70' y2='30' stroke='var(--ink)' stroke-width='0.8'/><polygon points='70,30 67,28 67,32' fill='var(--ink)'/><use href='badge:tick@50,8'/></svg>

options.name: metric-with-context
options.desc: A sparkline with a dashed baseline overlay calling out the SLO target.
options.art: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><use href='wf:spark-rising' x='5' y='8' width='90' height='40'/><use href='baseline:50@10,22,80?label=p99 target'/><text x='5' y='58' font-family='IBM Plex Mono' font-size='6' fill='var(--ink-2)'>last 7d</text></svg>

options.name: before-after
options.desc: Two states separated by divider:vs, with a times: multiplier annotating the delta.
options.art: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><use href='bars:[2,3,2,4,3]' x='0' y='6' width='40' height='40'/><use href='bars:[4,7,9,11,13]' x='60' y='6' width='40' height='40'/><use href='divider:vs@50'/><text x='20' y='55' text-anchor='middle' font-family='IBM Plex Mono' font-size='6' fill='var(--ink-2)'>before</text><text x='80' y='55' text-anchor='middle' font-family='IBM Plex Mono' font-size='6' fill='var(--ink-2)'>after</text></svg>
